---
title: pip
---

```{css css_custom, code=readLines("_css_custom.css"), echo=FALSE}
```
```{r knitr_config, code=readLines("_knitr_config.R"), echo=FALSE}
```

*********************************************************************

> 'preferred installer program' the standard package manager for python

*********************************************************************

* https://realpython.com/what-is-pip/  
* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/python-developer-set-up

*********************************************************************

## installation

### install

if you have installed python you should alredy have `pip`

see the link below if you dont have it installed:

* https://pip.pypa.io/en/stable/installing/

### check version

```{sh}
which pip
pip --version
```

### upgrade

```sh
pip install --upgrade pip
pip install -U pip # same as above
pip install -U pip setuptools wheel # same as above but what else it is doing?
```

### help

```sh
pip help
```

*********************************************************************

## pip install

```sh
pip install 'PackageName'
pip install 'PackageName==1.4'
pip install --upgrade PackageName 
pip uninstall PackageName
```

*********************************************************************

## pip freeze

```sh
# declare the dependencies in a requirements.txt file
pip freeze

# install dependencies from a requirements.txt file
pip install -r requirements.txt
```
